text.tspan: Canonical CBOR Payload — the exact bytes that get signed

text.font-family: 'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
text.tspan: Positional array of 7. No map, no keys, no ordering ambiguity. RFC 8949 §4.2 deterministic.

text.font-family: 'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
text.tspan: CBOR header byte: 0x87 (= major type 4 = array, short count = 7)

text.tspan: SHA-256 over the whole array bytes → event_hash (64 hex chars)

text.font-family: 'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
text.tspan: • Hex case ambiguity would make 'AB' and 'ab' distinct text but identical bytes.
• Genesis = empty bstr (0x40), byte-distinct from empty tstr (0x60). No ambiguity.

text.font-family: 'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
'Segoe Print', 'Bradley Hand', 'Comic Sans MS', 'Caveat', 'Patrick Hand', cursive, sans-serif
text.tspan: A map has key-ordering rules that libraries can drift on.
A positional array is canonical by construction — no keys exist to sort.
